column1: 2004-08-08窶撒he following question was supposedly given on a chemistry exam
column2: ﾃ｢竄ｬﾋ廬s hell exothermic (gives off heat) or endothermic (absorbs heat)? Support your answer with proof.ﾃ｢竄ｬ邃｢  Most of the students wrote proofs of their beliefs using Boyleﾃ｢竄ｬ邃｢s Law (gas cools when it expands and heats up when it is compressed) or some variant.  One student
column4: wrote the following:  ﾃ｢竄ｬﾋ廡irst we need to know how the mass of hell is changing in time. So
column5: we need to know the rate that souls are moving into hell and the rate they are leaving. I think that we can safely assume that once a soul gets to hell
column7: no souls are leaving.  ﾃ｢竄ｬﾋ廣s for how many souls are entering hell
column8: letﾃ｢竄ｬ邃｢s look at the different religions that exist in the world today. Some of these religions state that if you are not a member of their religion
column9: you will go to hell. Since there are more than one of these religions and since people do not belong to more than one religion
column10: we can project that all people and all souls go to hell.  ﾃ｢竄ｬﾋ弩ith birth and death rates as they are
column11: we can expect the number of souls in hell to increase exponentially. Now
column12: we look at the rate of change of the volume in hell because Boyleﾃ｢竄ｬ邃｢s Law states that in order for temperature and the pressure in hell to stay the same
column13: the volume of hell has to expand as souls are added. This gives two possibilities:  1.If hell is expanding at a slower rate than the rate at which souls enter hell
column14: then the temperature and pressure in hell will increase until all hell breaks loose.  2.Of course
column15: if hell is expanding at a rate faster than the increase of souls in hell
column16: then the temperature and pressure will drop until hell freezes over.  ﾃ｢竄ｬﾋ彜o which is it?  ﾃ｢竄ｬﾋ廬f we accept the postulate given to me by Ms Therese Banyan during my freshman year
column17: that ﾃ｢竄ｬﾅ妬t will be a cold night in hell before I sleep with youﾃ｢竄ｬ
